svg.text: swift run LLMEcosystemDemo — 16 packages, sixteen scenarios
== LLM Ecosystem Integration Demo ==
ProviderGatewayKit (routing) + StructuredOutputKit (decoding) + TokenMeterKit (cost) + ResponseCacheKit (caching) +
ToolRegistryKit (tool dispatch) + AgentLoopKit (agent loop) + GuardrailKit (PII redaction & policy) + TraceKit
(tracing & eval gates) + RetrievalKit (retrieval-augmented context) + PromptTemplateKit (prompt templating &
rollback) + RetryPolicyKit (rate limiting & retry policy) + ContextCompactionKit (conversation compaction under a
token budget) + AgentMemoryKit (long-term write/recall memory across sessions) + SemanticRouterKit (semantic
intent routing by embedding distance) + OutputRepairKit (bounded self-healing structured-output repair loop) +
StreamAggregatorKit (streamed delta + tool-call fragment reassembly)
[on-device provider, clean JSON] routed via on-device → decoded: WeatherReport(city: "Bengaluru", temperatureCelsius:
27.5, conditions: "cloudy")
[cloud provider, JSON fenced in prose] routed via cloud → decoded: WeatherReport(city: "Mumbai", temperatureCelsius:
31.0, conditions: "storm")
[self-hosted provider, self-repairing] decoded after a repair round-trip: WeatherReport(city: "Chennai",
temperatureCelsius: 33.2, conditions: "clear")
[cached scenario] attempt 1: MISS — routed via cloud, decoded: WeatherReport(city: "Pune", temperatureCelsius: 24.0,
conditions: "clear")
[cached scenario] attempt 2: HIT — no provider call, no additional cost

temperatureCelsius: 29.0, conditions: "clear")

[trace scenario] EvalGate passed: true (4 spans scored)
[retrieval scenario] query: "How does token metering and cost work in this ecosystem?"
[retrieval scenario] retrieved context:
[1] (score: 0.416) TokenMeterKit provides actor-based token usage and cost metering for LLM apps in Swift, tracking
cost per provider with Decimal-based pricing and a formatted cost report.
[2] (score: 0.239) GuardrailKit provides actor-based guardrails for Swift LLM apps, screening prompts and responses
for PII redaction and content policy violations before or after routing.
[retrieval scenario] routed via self-hosted → grounded answer: RAGAnswer(answer: "TokenMeterKit meters usage per
provider with Decimal-based pricing and a cost report.", sourceCount: 2)
[prompt template scenario] rendered v2 (strict):
You are a grounded assistant. Using ONLY the context provided, answer concisely and do not rely on anything outside
it.
Context:
TokenMeterKit meters usage per provider with Decimal-based pricing and a cost report.
Question: How is cost tracked across providers?
[prompt template scenario] routed via prompt-host → decoded: RAGAnswer(answer: "TokenMeterKit meters usage per
provider with Decimal-based pricing and a cost report.", sourceCount: 1)
[prompt template scenario] rolled back to v1
[prompt template scenario] rendered v1 (lenient, question omitted):
Answer the question using only the context below.
Context: TokenMeterKit meters usage per provider with Decimal-based pricing.
Question: {{question}}

rendered

[context compaction scenario] routed via compaction-host with compacted context → RAGAnswer(answer: "Keep all mutable
state behind actor isolation; cross concurrency domains only with Sendable.", sourceCount: 4)

is a senior iOS engineer buildi...
[agent memory scenario] routed via memory-host with recalled memory → RAGAnswer(answer: "A dark-themed, monospace-font
editor fits this user\'s stated preference.", sourceCount: 2)
[agent memory scenario] decay pass pruned 1 low-importance, unpinned memories

model router-host
